title: Bridge Essay: Unilateral Stimulation of the Dorsolateral Striatum Attenuates Goal-Directed Action.
concept: Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mechanicality** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
**Phase context:** Habit as automated behavior — loss of conscious control, automaticity, action monitoring.

**Unilateral Stimulation of the Dorsolateral Striatum Attenuates Goal-Directed Action.** — Eur J Neurosci (2025). DOI: 10.1111/ejn.70175. PMID: 40771101. It has long been known that the dorsomedial (DMS) and dorsolateral striatum (DLS) mediate distinct forms of action control, with DMS mediating goal-directed actions and DLS mediating habits. Recent evidence suggests that, in accord with its role in goal-directed control, unilateral stimulation of dorsomedial striatum (DMS) enhances actions contralateral to the stimulation in a manner that scales with the prior reward history of that action.
